title: phlox procumbens
intro: Phlox procumbens, also known as creeping phlox or wild sweet william, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to eastern North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its leaves and roots. The health benefits of phlox procumbens include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and congestion. Phlox procumbens is often prepared as a tea, salve, or tincture for its medicinal purposes, although it should be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
